code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Clean object values - remove quotes from strings
    def clean_value(val: str) -> str:
        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"'):
            return val[1:-1]
        return val

    # Group triples by subject to build coherent sentences
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Templates for predicates - all include subject for consistency
    templates = {
        "author": "{subject} was written by {object}",
        "isbnNumber": "{subject} has ISBN {object}",
        "followedBy": "{subject} is followed by {object}",
        "precededBy": "{subject} is preceded by {object}",
        "language": "{subject} is written in {object}",
        "mediaType": "{subject} is in {object} format",
        "numberOfPages": "{subject} contains {object} pages",
        "country": "{subject} is published in {object}",
        "publisher": "{subject} is published by {object}",
        "genre": "{subject} is a {object} work",
        "literaryGenre": "{subject} is in the {object} genre",
        "releaseDate": "{subject} was released on {object}",
        "birthDate": "{subject} was born on {object}",
        "spokenIn": "{object} is spoken in {subject}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{subject} is {object} ethnic group",
        "abbreviation": "{subject} is abbreviated as {object}",
        "firstPublicationYear": "{subject} was first published in {object}",
        "issnNumber": "{subject} has ISSN {object}",
        "codenCode": "{subject} has CODEN {object}",
        "LCCN_number": "{subject} has LCCN {object}",
        "eissnNumber": "{subject} has eISSN {object}",
        "frequency": "{subject} is published {object}",
        "academicDiscipline": "{subject} covers {object}",
        "editor": "{subject} is edited by {object}",
        "impactFactor": "{subject} has impact factor {object}",
        "oclcNumber": "{subject} has OCLC {object}",
        "libraryofCongressClassification": "{subject} is classified as {object}",
        "headquarter": "{subject} is headquartered in {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
        "affiliation": "{subject} is affiliated with {object}",
        "city": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "state": "{subject} is in {object} state",
        "influencedBy": "{subject} was influenced by {object}",
        "family": "{subject} is family {object}",
        "genus": "{subject} is genus {object}",
        "regionServed": "{subject} serves {object}",
        "founder": "{subject} was founded by {object}",
        "capital": "{subject} capital is {object}",
        "demonym": "{subject} is known as {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subject} leader title is {object}",
        "nickname": "{subject} is nicknamed {object}",
        "notableWork": "{subject} is known for {object}",
        "almaMater": "{subject} attended {object}",
        "doctoralAdvisor": "{subject}'s advisor was {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} is from {object}",
        "residence": "{subject} resides in {object}",
        "parentCompany": "{subject} is subsidiary of {object}",
    }

    # Build main subject clause
    main_subject = triples[0].subject if triples else ""
    main_clauses = []
    other_clauses = []

    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clean_obj = clean_value(t.object)
            clause = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=clean_obj)
        else:
            clause = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {clean_value(t.object)}"

        # Identify main work subject vs related subjects
        if t.subject == main_subject:
            main_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            other_clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all clauses into one flowing sentence
    all_clauses = main_clauses + other_clauses

    if len(all_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = all_clauses[0]
    elif len(all_clauses) == 2:
        sentence = all_clauses[0] + ", and " + all_clauses[1]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(all_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + all_clauses[-1]

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
